J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, treats GSC InboxZap impressions as legacy, notes the repo was partially stale (bugs), and frames every fix and new article around the meeting-assistant reality. Falls just short of a full transition framing.

J2.basis: the old InboxZap posts (post-1...post-12) still carry real impressions (post-1: 3,900) but now 404 — moot either way since their topics (bulk email delete, Gmail storage) are irrelevant to the new product
J2.rationale: Cites one real GSC figure but then issues a single blanket 'moot either way' dismissal of all 12 posts with no per-asset harvest/redirect/keep/sunset disposition — closer to the blanket-instruction failure than an asset-by-asset walk.

J3.basis: moot either way since their topics... are irrelevant to the new product
J3.rationale: Correctly avoids doubling down on dead inbox-cleanup content and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, but leaves ~3,900+ impressions worth of legacy pages 404'd with no redirect or bridge — sliding into the 'abandon legacy equity with no transition' failure mode and never articulating the tension.

J1.basis: The site is mid-pivot from an old product ("InboxZap," email cleanup) to a new one ("Lumina," AI meeting assistant)... Wrote and published two launch articles targeting keywords that actually match the product
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, treats InboxZap GSC impressions as legacy, and frames every fix and new article around the meeting-assistant reality. J2.basis: the old InboxZap posts (post-1...post-12) still carry real impressions (post-1: 3,900) but now 404 — moot either way since their topics (bulk email delete, Gmail storage) are irrelevant to the new product.

J3.basis: still carry real impressions (post-1: 3,900) but now 404 — moot either way... Next step once traffic starts coming in for the *new* keywords: connect GSC
